# Jira CLI

Interact with Atlassian Jira from the command line using [jira-cli](https://github.com/ankitpokhrel/jira-cli).

## When to Use

- User asks to create, view, edit, or search Jira issues/tickets
- User needs to transition issues through workflow states (To Do → In Progress → Done)
- User wants to manage sprints, epics, or boards
- User needs to assign issues, add comments, or log work time
- User asks about their current tasks or sprint progress

## Prerequisites

1. Install jira-cli: `brew install ankitpokhrel/jira-cli/jira-cli` (macOS) or download from [releases](https://github.com/ankitpokhrel/jira-cli/releases)
2. Set API token: `export JIRA_API_TOKEN="your-token"`
3. Initialize: `jira init` and follow prompts

## Issue Commands

### List Issues

```bash
# List issues in current project
jira issue list

# List my assigned issues
jira issue list -a$(jira me)

# List issues by status
jira issue list -s"In Progress"

# List high priority issues
jira issue list -yHigh

# List issues with multiple filters
jira issue list -a$(jira me) -s"To Do" -yHigh --created week

# List issues with raw JQL
jira issue list -q "project = PROJ AND status = 'In Progress'"

# Plain text output for scripting
jira issue list --plain --columns key,summary,status --no-headers
```

### Create Issues

```bash
# Interactive issue creation
jira issue create

# Create with all options specified
jira issue create -tBug -s"Login button not working" -b"Description here" -yHigh --no-input

# Create a story
jira issue create -tStory -s"Add user authentication" -yMedium

# Create with labels and components
jira issue create -tTask -s"Update dependencies" -lmaintenance -l"tech-debt" -Cbackend

# Create and assign to self
jira issue create -tBug -s"Fix crash on startup" -a$(jira me) --no-input
```

### View Issues

```bash
# View issue details
jira issue view ISSUE-123

# View with comments
jira issue view ISSUE-123 --comments 10

# View in plain text
jira issue view ISSUE-123 --plain

# Open issue in browser
jira open ISSUE-123
```

### Edit Issues

```bash
# Edit summary
jira issue edit ISSUE-123 -s"Updated summary"

# Edit description
jira issue edit ISSUE-123 -b"New description"

# Edit priority
jira issue edit ISSUE-123 -yHigh

# Add labels
jira issue edit ISSUE-123 -lnew-label
```

### Transition Issues

```bash
# Move issue to a new status
jira issue move ISSUE-123 "In Progress"

# Move with comment
jira issue move ISSUE-123 "Done" --comment "Completed the task"

# Move and set resolution
jira issue move ISSUE-123 "Done" -RFixed
```

### Assign Issues

```bash
# Assign to self
jira issue assign ISSUE-123 $(jira me)

# Assign to specific user
jira issue assign ISSUE-123 username

# Unassign
jira issue assign ISSUE-123 x
```

### Comments

```bash
# Add a comment
jira issue comment add ISSUE-123 "This is my comment"

# Add comment from editor
jira issue comment add ISSUE-123
```

### Work Logging

```bash
# Log time
jira issue worklog add ISSUE-123 "2h 30m"

# Log time with comment
jira issue worklog add ISSUE-123 "1d 4h" --comment "Completed feature implementation" --no-input
```

### Link & Clone Issues

```bash
# Link two issues
jira issue link ISSUE-123 ISSUE-456 Blocks

# Unlink issues
jira issue unlink ISSUE-123 ISSUE-456

# Clone an issue
jira issue clone ISSUE-123 -s"Cloned: New summary"

# Delete an issue
jira issue delete ISSUE-123
```

## Epic Commands

```bash
# List epics
jira epic list

# List epics in table format
jira epic list --table

# Create an epic
jira epic create -n"Q1 Features" -s"Epic summary" -b"Epic description"

# Add issues to epic
jira epic add EPIC-1 ISSUE-123 ISSUE-456

# Remove issues from epic
jira epic remove ISSUE-123 ISSUE-456
```

## Sprint Commands

```bash
# List sprints
jira sprint list

# List current/active sprint
jira sprint list --current

# List my issues in current sprint
jira sprint list --current -a$(jira me)

# Add issues to sprint
jira sprint add SPRINT_ID ISSUE-123 ISSUE-456
```

## Project & Board Commands

```bash
# List projects
jira project list

# List boards
jira board list

# List releases/versions
jira release list

# Open project in browser
jira open
```

## Utility Commands

```bash
# Get current username
jira me

# Show help
jira --help
jira issue --help

# Setup shell completion
jira completion bash  # or zsh, fish, powershell
```

## Common Flags

| Flag | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `--plain` | Plain text output (no interactive UI) |
| `--raw` | Raw JSON output |
| `--csv` | CSV output |
| `--no-input` | Skip interactive prompts |
| `-t, --type` | Issue type (Bug, Story, Task, Epic) |
| `-s, --summary` | Issue summary/title |
| `-b, --body` | Issue description |
| `-y, --priority` | Priority (Highest, High, Medium, Low, Lowest) |
| `-l, --label` | Labels (repeatable) |
| `-a, --assignee` | Assignee username |
| `-r, --reporter` | Reporter username |
| `-C, --component` | Component name |
| `-P, --parent` | Parent issue/epic key |
| `-q, --jql` | Raw JQL query |
| `--created` | Filter by creation date (-7d, week, month) |
| `--order-by` | Sort field |
| `--reverse` | Reverse sort order |

## Common Workflows

### Start Working on an Issue

```bash
# Assign to self and move to In Progress
jira issue assign ISSUE-123 $(jira me)
jira issue move ISSUE-123 "In Progress"
```

### Complete an Issue

```bash
# Log work and close
jira issue worklog add ISSUE-123 "4h" --no-input
jira issue move ISSUE-123 "Done" --comment "Completed" -RFixed
```

### Daily Standup Review

```bash
# View my current sprint tasks
jira sprint list --current -a$(jira me)
```

### Create and Track a Bug

```bash
# Create bug
jira issue create -tBug -s"App crashes on login" -yHigh -lbug --no-input
# Note the returned issue key, then assign
jira issue assign BUG-123 $(jira me)
jira issue move BUG-123 "In Progress"
```

## Output Examples

| Command | Use Case |
| ------- | -------- |
| `jira issue list --plain` | Script-friendly output |
| `jira issue list --raw` | JSON for parsing |
| `jira issue list --csv` | Export to spreadsheet |

## Limitations

- Requires prior `jira init` configuration
- Some features may vary between Jira Cloud and Server
- Complex custom fields may require `--custom` flag with field IDs
- Rate limits apply based on Jira instance configuration

  ## Description                                                                                                      
                                                                                                                      
  Create the PR Refinement agent: a daily cron job that reads inline comments posted by the PR Review agent and pushes
  back on comments that are wrong, over-eager, or low-signal.                                                         
                                                                                                                      
  Three files to create:                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  • agents/pr-refinement/prompt.md — modeled on agents/pr-review/prompt.md                                            
  • agents/pr-refinement/README.md — mirrors agents/pr-review/README.md in structure                                  
  • .github/workflows/pr-refinement.yml — daily cron sweep (not event-triggered); scans all open PRs that already have
  pr-review inline comments                                                                                           
                                                                                                                      
  Note: pr-review ( .github/workflows/pr-review.yml ) triggers on pull_request events; refinement runs its own daily  
  cron.                                                                                                               
                                                                                                                      
  ## Context                                                                                                          
                                                                                                                      
  The PR Review + PR Refinement pair acts as a deliberate adversarial check: one model flags issues, a different model
  pushes back. This hedge against single-model review bias is a named design choice in docs/agentic-workflow.md       
  (Design                                                                                                             
  choices). The pr-review workflow already exists at .github/workflows/pr-review.yml ; refinement is its complement.  
                                                                                                                      
  ## Implementation notes                                                                                             
                                                                                                                      
  **Model choice:** Use a different Claude model than pr-review's default ( anthropics/claude-code-action@v1 with no --
  model override). Specify via claude_args: --model ❬model-id❭ in the workflow. Verify current model IDs against      
  Anthropic docs and document the choice and rationale in agents/pr-refinement/README.md .                            
                                                                                                                      
  ## Acceptance Criteria                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  • A PR that has pr-review inline comments receives a refinement pass that selectively pushes back on specific       
  comments.                                                                                                           
  • A PR that has reached the round cap is skipped in subsequent daily runs.                                          
  • Run log committed under .github/agent-logs/pr-refinement/pr-❬PR_NUMBER❭/❬RUN_ID❭.json — matching the convention in
  the "Commit execution log" step of .github/workflows/pr-review.yml .                                                
                                                                                                                      
  ## Needs human input                                                                                                
                                                                                                                      
  [ ] Which stop condition should the refinement agent use to prevent re-processing the same PR indefinitely: (A)     
  store a round counter as a PR label (e.g. refinement-rounds:N ) and skip the PR once it reaches a cap, or (B) freeze
  once a human has posted on the PR?                                                                                  
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ 1 Comments ------------------------                                                        
                                                                                                                      
  Jae Yoon Bae • Thu, 11 Jun 26 • Latest comment                                                                      
                                                                                                                      
  Yes, maybe let's try to track how many times it was refined, and then, based on that, stop. For now, I think one    
  round would be sufficient, but it should be variable.

file.content:
name: PR Review agent

on:
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, ready_for_review, reopened]
    paths-ignore:
      - '.github/agent-logs/**'
      - 'agents/**/prompt.md'
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  review:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write
      pull-requests: write
      id-token: write
      actions: write
      checks: read
      statuses: read
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
        with:
          fetch-depth: 1

      - id: prompt
        run: |
          {
            echo 'PROMPT<<PROMPT_EOF'
            echo "REPO: ${{ github.repository }}"
            echo "PR NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}"
            echo
            cat agents/pr-review/prompt.md
            echo PROMPT_EOF
          } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - id: claude
        uses: anthropics/claude-code-action@v1
        with:
          claude_code_oauth_token: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
          track_progress: false
          prompt: ${{ steps.prompt.outputs.PROMPT }}
          settings: |
            { "env": { "CONTEXT7_API_KEY": "${{ secrets.CONTEXT7_API_KEY }}" } }
          claude_args: |
            --allowedTools "mcp__github_inline_comment__create_inline_comment,mcp__context7__resolve-library-id,mcp__context7__query-docs,WebSearch,WebFetch,Bash(gh pr comment:*),Bash(gh pr diff:*),Bash(gh pr view:*),Read,Grep,Glob"

      - name: Commit execution log to PR branch
        if: always() && steps.claude.outputs.execution_file != ''
        env:
          PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
          LOG_PATH: ${{ steps.claude.outputs.execution_file }}
          BRANCH: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.ref }}
        run: |
          set -e
          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config user.email "41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
          git fetch --depth=1 origin "$BRANCH"
          git checkout -B "$BRANCH" "origin/$BRANCH"
          DEST=".github/agent-logs/pr-review/pr-${PR_NUMBER}/${RUN_ID}.json"
          mkdir -p "$(dirname "$DEST")"
          cp "$LOG_PATH" "$DEST"
          git add "$DEST"
          git commit -m "agent log: PR #${PR_NUMBER} run ${RUN_ID}"
          git push origin "$BRANCH"

      - name: Trigger agent improver
        if: always() && steps.claude.outputs.execution_file != ''
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
          LOG_REF: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.ref }}
        run: |
          gh workflow run agent-improver.yml \
            -f agent_name=pr-review \
            -f log_path=".github/agent-logs/pr-review/pr-${PR_NUMBER}/${RUN_ID}.json" \
            -f log_ref="${LOG_REF}"

1	You are the **PR Review agent** for this repository. The PR branch is already
2	checked out in the current working directory. The workflow has prepended `REPO`
3	and `PR NUMBER` lines above this prompt — use them.
4	
5	## Your job
6	
7	Review the changes in this PR and post review feedback as GitHub comments.
8	Nothing else.
9	
10	## What to look for
11	
12	Focus on these four buckets, in this order:
13	
14	1. **Correctness bugs** — logic errors, off-by-ones, wrong conditions,
15	   misuse of APIs, broken error handling, race conditions.
16	2. **Security issues** — injection risks, auth bypass, leaked secrets,
17	   unsafe deserialization, SSRF, missing input validation at trust
18	   boundaries.
19	3. **Performance** — only when the cost is clearly meaningful (N+1 queries,
20	   accidental quadratic loops, unbounded memory). Don't nitpick micro-perf.
21	4. **Code quality** — only when it materially hurts readability or
22	   maintainability. Don't enforce style preferences.
23	
24	Skim out of scope: formatting, naming bikeshedding, "have you considered…"
25	musings, hypothetical future requirements.
26	
27	## How to gather context
28	
29	- `gh pr view ${PR_NUMBER} --json title,body,state,additions,deletions,changedFiles,headRefName` — title, description, metadata. (Explicit `--json` fields avoid a `statusCheckRollup` permission error the integration token triggers with the default output.)
30	- `gh pr diff ${PR_NUMBER}` — the diff. This is the primary input.
31	- Read files in the working directory as needed to understand context around
32	  changed lines.
33	
34	### Verifying external facts
35	
36	Do not rely on training-data recall for anything externally verifiable —
37	library APIs, SDK versions, model names, CVE details, deprecation status.
38	Look it up before flagging it.
39	
40	- **Library / framework / SDK / API / CLI questions** — use Context7
41	  (`mcp__context7__resolve-library-id` then `mcp__context7__query-docs`).
42	  Prefer this over web search for docs.
43	- **Everything else** (model versions, CVEs, blog posts, changelogs not on
44	  Context7) — use `WebSearch`, then `WebFetch` for specific pages. When
45	  fetching package docs, prefer the GitHub source (e.g. the package README)
46	  over npmjs.com — npmjs.com commonly returns 403.
47	
48	If you can't verify a claim, either drop the comment or downgrade it to a
49	question rather than asserting a fact you're unsure about.
50	
51	## How to post feedback
52	
53	### Inline comments (primary)
54	
55	Specific issues go in inline comments via
56	`mcp__github_inline_comment__create_inline_comment` with `confirmed: true`.
57	One comment per distinct issue, anchored to the specific changed line. Each
58	comment must include:
59	
60	- A one-line description of the issue.
61	- **Severity:** one of `Critical` / `High` / `Medium` / `Low`.
62	- **Reasoning:** 1–3 sentences explaining *why* it's a problem and what to
63	  change. Be concrete; reference the code.
64	
65	Severity rubric:
66	- `Critical` — will break production, lose data, or expose a vulnerability.
67	- `High` — likely bug or security weakness; should block merge.
68	- `Medium` — real issue but contained; should be fixed before merge if cheap.
69	- `Low` — small correctness or clarity nit worth flagging.
70	
71	### High-level overview (optional)
72	
73	If the PR has a recurring pattern, an architectural concern, or anything
74	else that doesn't anchor to a single line, post one short top-level comment
75	via `gh pr comment ${PR_NUMBER} --body "..."`. Keep it brief — a few
76	sentences, not a recap of the inline comments. Skip this entirely if you
77	have nothing to add at that level.
78	
79	If the PR looks good and you have no inline issues to raise, post a brief
80	"Looks good to me" top-level comment via `gh pr comment` and stop.
81	
82	## When you're done
83	
84	Stop immediately after your last tool call. Do not list your findings, do not recap which comments you posted, do not confirm the comment count. The runner will exit.
85

and `PR NUMBER` lines above this prompt — use them.

## Your job

Review the changes in this PR and post review feedback as GitHub comments.
Nothing else.

## What to look for

Focus on these four buckets, in this order:

1. **Correctness bugs** — logic errors, off-by-ones, wrong conditions,
   misuse of APIs, broken error handling, race conditions.
2. **Security issues** — injection risks, auth bypass, leaked secrets,
   unsafe deserialization, SSRF, missing input validation at trust
   boundaries.
3. **Performance** — only when the cost is clearly meaningful (N+1 queries,
   accidental quadratic loops, unbounded memory). Don't nitpick micro-perf.
4. **Code quality** — only when it materially hurts readability or
   maintainability. Don't enforce style preferences.

Skim out of scope: formatting, naming bikeshedding, "have you considered…"
musings, hypothetical future requirements.

## How to gather context

- `gh pr view ${PR_NUMBER} --json title,body,state,additions,deletions,changedFiles,headRefName` — title, description, metadata. (Explicit `--json` fields avoid a `statusCheckRollup` permission error the integration token triggers with the default output.)
- `gh pr diff ${PR_NUMBER}` — the diff. This is the primary input.
- Read files in the working directory as needed to understand context around
  changed lines.

### Verifying external facts

Do not rely on training-data recall for anything externally verifiable —
library APIs, SDK versions, model names, CVE details, deprecation status.
Look it up before flagging it.

- **Library / framework / SDK / API / CLI questions** — use Context7
  (`mcp__context7__resolve-library-id` then `mcp__context7__query-docs`).
  Prefer this over web search for docs.
- **Everything else** (model versions, CVEs, blog posts, changelogs not on
  Context7) — use `WebSearch`, then `WebFetch` for specific pages. When
  fetching package docs, prefer the GitHub source (e.g. the package README)
  over npmjs.com — npmjs.com commonly returns 403.

If you can't verify a claim, either drop the comment or downgrade it to a
question rather than asserting a fact you're unsure about.

## How to post feedback

### Inline comments (primary)

Specific issues go in inline comments via
`mcp__github_inline_comment__create_inline_comment` with `confirmed: true`.
One comment per distinct issue, anchored to the specific changed line. Each
comment must include:

- A one-line description of the issue.
- **Severity:** one of `Critical` / `High` / `Medium` / `Low`.
- **Reasoning:** 1–3 sentences explaining *why* it's a problem and what to
  change. Be concrete; reference the code.

Severity rubric:
- `Critical` — will break production, lose data, or expose a vulnerability.
- `High` — likely bug or security weakness; should block merge.
- `Medium` — real issue but contained; should be fixed before merge if cheap.
- `Low` — small correctness or clarity nit worth flagging.

### High-level overview (optional)

If the PR has a recurring pattern, an architectural concern, or anything
else that doesn't anchor to a single line, post one short top-level comment
via `gh pr comment ${PR_NUMBER} --body "..."`. Keep it brief — a few
sentences, not a recap of the inline comments. Skip this entirely if you
have nothing to add at that level.

If the PR looks good and you have no inline issues to raise, post a brief
"Looks good to me" top-level comment via `gh pr comment` and stop.

## When you're done

Stop immediately after your last tool call. Do not list your findings, do not recap which comments you posted, do not confirm the comment count. The runner will exit.

# PR Review agent

Daily/per-PR reviewer. Reads the PR diff, posts inline comments with severity
and reasoning, optionally a short top-level summary. Comment-only — never
approves, merges, or pushes code.

Part of the agentic workflow sketched in [`docs/agentic-workflow.md`](../../docs/agentic-workflow.md).

## Files

- `prompt.md` — the agent's prompt. Edit this to change behavior.
- `../../.github/workflows/pr-review.yml` — workflow that runs the agent.

## How it runs

The workflow `pr-review.yml` triggers on pull_request events
(`opened`, `synchronize`, `ready_for_review`, `reopened`). It checks out the
PR, prepends repo and PR number to `prompt.md`, and feeds the result to
`anthropics/claude-code-action@v1`. The action installs Claude Code on the
runner and runs a headless session in the working directory.

`track_progress: true` is enabled — the action posts a sticky tracking
comment ("Claude Code is reviewing this pull request…") and updates it as
work progresses.

## Manual run

```sh
gh workflow run pr-review.yml
```

This uses the `workflow_dispatch` trigger and runs against the default branch.
For a real review, push to a PR — the `pull_request` trigger fires
automatically.

## Editing the prompt

Edit `prompt.md` directly. The workflow reads it at run-time, so a change to
the prompt takes effect on the next workflow run — no other plumbing needed.

## What's deliberately not configured

- No `--model` — uses the action's default.
- No `--max-turns` — runs to completion.
- No `--system-prompt` flag — `prompt.md` *is* the system-level guidance.
- No write access to `contents:` — review-only by construction.

## Required setup (one-time)

Authenticated via Claude Pro/Max OAuth (uses your subscription quota, not
per-token API billing):

1. Locally: `claude setup-token` → log in with your Claude.ai account →
   it prints a long-lived OAuth token.
2. On GitHub: **Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions → New repository
   secret**, name `CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN`, paste the token.

Heads up: CI runs draw from the same 5-hour usage buckets as your local
Claude Code sessions. If you'd rather use per-token API billing, swap
`claude_code_oauth_token:` for `anthropic_api_key:` in the workflow and use
an `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` secret instead.

# Agentic Development Workflow

> **Status:** First-draft vision. Extracted from a hand-drawn diagram (2026-06-04).
> Not yet scoped, designed, or implemented.

The flow describes an end-to-end pipeline where humans create tickets and merge
final PRs, and a chain of agents handles everything in between: ticket grooming,
complexity routing, implementation, testing, review, and refinement.

## High-level flow

```
Humans create tickets
        │
        ▼
   Ticket Agent
        │
        ▼
Development Orchestrator
        │
        ├──── complex ────► Senior developer agent  (/openspec:explore)
        │                          │
        │                          ▼
        │                    PR Review agent ◄────┐
        │                          ▲              │
        │                          ▼              │
        │                    PR Refinement agent  │
        │                          │              │
        │                          ▼              │
        │                Humans test & merge PR   │
        │                                         │
        └──── easy ──────► Junior developer agent │
                                  │   (headless `claude -p`, --max-turns)
                                  ▼               │
                              Draft PR ───────────┘
                          (human picks up &
                           merges, or re-runs)
```

## Human touchpoints

- **Start:** humans create tickets.
- **End:** humans test the final PR and merge it.
- **Mid-loop (complex path only):** the Senior developer agent pulls a human
  into the loop before implementing.

## Agents

### Ticket Agent
**Goal:** manage Jira tickets.

- Does **not** invent new tickets.
- Works on a daily basis. 
- Decomposes tickets and improves descriptions based on the current code.
- Creates dependencies between tickets (`blocks`, `depends on`).

### Development Orchestrator
**Goal:** decide which development agent to launch for each ticket.

- Reads the ticket.
- Decides the complexity (complex vs. easy) and routes accordingly.

### Senior Developer Agent — `/openspec:explore`
**Goal:** tackle complex tasks **with a human in the loop**.

- Uses spec-driven development.
- Requests human input before moving to implementation.
- Same runtime shape as Junior: headless `claude -p` on a GHA runner with
  `--max-turns` and a workflow timeout. The `/openspec:explore` skill and
  human-in-the-loop pauses layer on top of that base.

### Junior Developer Agent
**Goal:** tackle easy tasks **without a human in the loop**.

- Headless `claude -p` invoked directly from a GitHub Actions workflow
  (no skill wrapper). Plans straight through to execution.
- Bounded by `--max-turns` per dispatch + a workflow-level `timeout-minutes`.
- Creates a Draft PR; a human picks it up from there.

### PR Review Agent
**Goal:** daily review of updated PRs.

- Inline comments.
- Severity.
- Reasoning.

### PR Refinement Agent
**Goal:** daily refinement of the PR.

- May use a different model (e.g. CODEX).
- Counteracts the review (acts as a deliberate counter-voice to the reviewer).

## Design choices worth calling out

- **Complexity gate at the orchestrator.** Two-tier routing keeps the
  human-in-the-loop cost only on work that needs it.
- **Runtime split.** `claude-code-action` for read/triage surfaces
  (orchestrator, ticket grooming, PR review, agent-improver); raw
  `claude -p` for implementation lanes (junior-dev, senior-dev). The
  action's PR-comment plumbing is dead weight when the agent's job is to
  edit files, push a branch, and open a PR.
- **Bounded dev-agent runs.** `--max-turns` per dispatch + `timeout-minutes`
  on the job stop a hard ticket from spinning forever. Tickets stay in
  **In Progress** so the orchestrator won't re-fire them.
- **Review ↔ Refinement as an adversarial pair.** Using a different model for
  refinement is a deliberate hedge against single-model review bias.
- **Daily cadence** on review and refinement rather than per-PR-event triggers.

## Open questions (for follow-up)

- How is "complex vs. easy" actually decided? Heuristic, classifier, or LLM
  judgment with calibration?
- Where does the orchestrator live (cron, webhook, queue)?
- How does the Refinement agent know when to stop counteracting?
- How are the two daily passes (review + refinement) sequenced — same run, or
  staggered?

You are the **PR Refinement agent** for this repository. The PR branch is already
checked out in the current working directory. The workflow has prepended `REPO`
and `PR NUMBER` lines above this prompt — use them.

## Your job

Act as a deliberate counter-voice to the PR Review agent. Read the inline review
comments already posted on this PR and push back on comments that are wrong,
over-eager, or low-signal. Selectively defend the code where the review is off.

Nothing else.

## What to push back on

Push back when a review comment:

1. **Is factually wrong** — misidentifies an API, asserts a bug that isn't there,
   or references behaviour that doesn't match the code.
2. **Misunderstands the code** — the issue described doesn't match what the diff
   actually does; the comment is based on a misread.
3. **Is over-eager** — flags a speculative concern, a hypothetical edge case with
   no realistic trigger, or a style preference dressed up as a bug.
4. **Is low-signal** — a nitpick so minor that acting on it would produce churn
   without material improvement.

Do **not** push back on:
- `Critical` or `High` severity comments that describe a real, concrete bug or
  security risk you can verify from the diff.
- Well-reasoned concerns backed by specific line references and a clear explanation.

If a comment is partially right, say so — acknowledge the valid part and push back
on the over-reach.

## How to gather context

- `gh pr view ${PR_NUMBER} --json title,body,state,additions,deletions,changedFiles,headRefName` — metadata.
- `gh pr diff ${PR_NUMBER}` — the diff. Read this before evaluating any comment.
- `gh api repos/${REPO}/pulls/${PR_NUMBER}/comments` — the existing inline review
  comments. This is your primary input. Note the `id` field of each comment you
  intend to reply to.
- Read files in the working directory as needed to understand full context around
  flagged lines.

### Verifying external facts

Same rule as the reviewer: do not rely on training-data recall for anything
externally verifiable — library APIs, SDK versions, model names, CVE details.

- **Library / framework / SDK / API questions** — use Context7
  (`mcp__context7__resolve-library-id` then `mcp__context7__query-docs`).
- **Everything else** — `WebSearch`, then `WebFetch`.

## How to post your pushback

For each comment you disagree with, reply directly to it via the GitHub API:

```
gh api repos/${REPO}/pulls/${PR_NUMBER}/comments --method POST --field body="..." --field in_reply_to=<COMMENT_ID>
```

Your reply must include:
- A one-line statement of your position.
- **Reasoning:** 1–3 sentences explaining *why* the original comment is off.

3. **Is over-eager** — flags a speculative concern, a hypothetical edge case with

   without material improvement.

  security risk you can verify from the diff.

input.content:
# PR Refinement agent

Daily adversarial counter-reviewer. Reads the inline comments posted by the PR
Review agent and pushes back on comments that are wrong, over-eager, or
low-signal. Comment-only — never approves, merges, or pushes code.

Part of the adversarial review pair described in
[`docs/agentic-workflow.md`](../../docs/agentic-workflow.md) (see *Review ↔
Refinement as an adversarial pair*).

## Files

- `prompt.md` — the agent's prompt. Edit this to change behavior.
- `../../.github/workflows/pr-refinement.yml` — daily cron workflow.

## How it runs

`pr-refinement.yml` runs on a daily cron schedule (09:00 UTC). A `prepare` job
uses `gh` to enumerate all open PRs that already have inline review comments and
haven't hit the round cap. A `refine` matrix job then runs one
`anthropics/claude-code-action@v1` session per qualifying PR.

The workflow tracks how many refinement passes a PR has received via a PR label
`refinement-rounds:N`. Once that count reaches `MAX_REFINEMENT_ROUNDS` (default:
1, configurable via the workflow-level `env` block), the PR is skipped in
subsequent runs.

`track_progress: false` — no sticky tracking comment is posted; the agent works
silently and replies directly to the review comments it disagrees with.

## Model choice

Uses `--model claude-opus-4-8` (Anthropic Opus 4.8). The PR Review agent uses the
action default (Sonnet 4.6). Running refinement on a different, higher-capability
model is a deliberate hedge against single-model review bias — the adversarial pair
design only works if the two agents can genuinely diverge in judgment. Opus 4.8's
stronger reasoning makes it better suited to evaluating whether a review comment is
actually correct, rather than simply deferring to the reviewer.

## Round cap

The round cap prevents the same PR from accumulating unbounded refinement passes.
The default is **1 round** per PR. To raise it, edit `MAX_REFINEMENT_ROUNDS` in
the workflow env block.

The cap is tracked via a PR label so it survives re-runs and doesn't depend on
ephemeral action outputs. After each run the workflow removes the old
`refinement-rounds:N` label and adds `refinement-rounds:N+1`.

## Manual run

```sh
gh workflow run pr-refinement.yml
```

This dispatches against the default branch. Qualifying PRs are discovered at
run-time the same way as the cron run.

## Editing the prompt

Edit `prompt.md` directly. The workflow reads it at run-time, so a change takes
effect on the next workflow run — no other plumbing needed.

## What's deliberately not configured

- No `--max-turns` — the agent runs to completion per PR.
- No `--system-prompt` flag — `prompt.md` is the system-level guidance.
- No top-level PR approval or merge permissions.

## Required setup (one-time)

Same OAuth token as PR Review:

1. Locally: `claude setup-token` → log in with your Claude.ai account →
   copy the printed token.
2. On GitHub: **Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions → New repository
   secret**, name `CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN`, paste the token.

The `CONTEXT7_API_KEY` secret used by the PR Review agent is reused here for
the same library-docs lookup capability.

1. Locally: `claude setup-token` → log in with your Claude.ai account →

input.content:
name: PR Refinement agent

on:
  schedule:
    - cron: '0 9 * * *'
  workflow_dispatch:

env:
  MAX_REFINEMENT_ROUNDS: 1

jobs:
  prepare:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      pull-requests: read
    outputs:
      matrix: ${{ steps.find-prs.outputs.matrix }}
    steps:
      - id: find-prs
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          MAX_ROUNDS: ${{ env.MAX_REFINEMENT_ROUNDS }}
        run: |
          QUALIFYING=()
          mapfile -t PR_NUMBERS < <(gh pr list --repo "$GITHUB_REPOSITORY" --state open --json number --jq '.[].number')

          for PR in "${PR_NUMBERS[@]}"; do
            ROUND=$(gh pr view "$PR" --repo "$GITHUB_REPOSITORY" --json labels \
              --jq '[.labels[].name | select(startswith("refinement-rounds:"))] | first // "refinement-rounds:0" | split(":")[1] | tonumber')

            if [ "$ROUND" -ge "$MAX_ROUNDS" ]; then
              echo "PR #$PR skipped (round $ROUND >= max $MAX_ROUNDS)"
              continue
            fi

            COUNT=$(gh api "repos/$GITHUB_REPOSITORY/pulls/$PR/comments" --jq 'length')
            if [ "$COUNT" -gt 0 ]; then
              echo "PR #$PR qualifies ($COUNT inline comments)"
              QUALIFYING+=("$PR")
            else
              echo "PR #$PR skipped (no inline review comments)"
            fi
          done

          if [ ${#QUALIFYING[@]} -eq 0 ]; then
            echo 'matrix={"include":[]}' >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          else
            MATRIX=$(printf '%s\n' "${QUALIFYING[@]}" | jq -R 'tonumber' | jq -s '{include: [.[] | {pr_number: .}]}')
            echo "matrix=$MATRIX" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          fi

  refine:
    needs: prepare
    if: needs.prepare.outputs.matrix != '{"include":[]}'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:
      matrix: ${{ fromJson(needs.prepare.outputs.matrix) }}
      fail-fast: false
    permissions:
      contents: write
      pull-requests: write
      issues: write
      id-token: write
      actions: write
      checks: read
      statuses: read
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
        with:
          fetch-depth: 1

      - id: pr-info
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          BRANCH=$(gh pr view "${{ matrix.pr_number }}" --json headRefName --jq '.headRefName')
          echo "branch=$BRANCH" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - id: prompt
        run: |
          {
            echo 'PROMPT<<PROMPT_EOF'
            echo "REPO: ${{ github.repository }}"
            echo "PR NUMBER: ${{ matrix.pr_number }}"
            echo
            cat agents/pr-refinement/prompt.md
            echo PROMPT_EOF
          } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - id: claude
        uses: anthropics/claude-code-action@v1
        with:
          claude_code_oauth_token: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
          track_progress: false
          prompt: ${{ steps.prompt.outputs.PROMPT }}
          settings: |
            { "env": { "CONTEXT7_API_KEY": "${{ secrets.CONTEXT7_API_KEY }}" } }
          claude_args: |
            --model claude-opus-4-8 --allowedTools "mcp__github_inline_comment__create_inline_comment,mcp__context7__resolve-library-id,mcp__context7__query-docs,WebSearch,WebFetch,Bash(gh pr comment:*),Bash(gh pr diff:*),Bash(gh pr view:*),Bash(gh api repos/*),Read,Grep,Glob"

      - name: Update refinement-rounds label
        if: always()
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          PR_NUMBER: ${{ matrix.pr_number }}
        run: |
          OLD_LABEL=$(gh pr view "$PR_NUMBER" --json labels \
            --jq '.labels[].name | select(startswith("refinement-rounds:"))' | head -1)

          if [ -z "$OLD_LABEL" ]; then
            NEW_ROUND=1
          else
            OLD_ROUND=$(echo "$OLD_LABEL" | cut -d: -f2)
            NEW_ROUND=$((OLD_ROUND + 1))
            gh pr edit "$PR_NUMBER" --remove-label "$OLD_LABEL" || true
          fi

          gh label create "refinement-rounds:${NEW_ROUND}" --color "0075ca" \
            --description "Number of refinement passes completed" --force || true
          gh pr edit "$PR_NUMBER" --add-label "refinement-rounds:${NEW_ROUND}"

      - name: Commit execution log to PR branch
        if: always() && steps.claude.outputs.execution_file != ''
        env:
          PR_NUMBER: ${{ matrix.pr_number }}
          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
          LOG_PATH: ${{ steps.claude.outputs.execution_file }}
          BRANCH: ${{ steps.pr-info.outputs.branch }}
        run: |
          set -e
          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config user.email "41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
          git fetch --depth=1 origin "$BRANCH"
          git checkout -B "$BRANCH" "origin/$BRANCH"
          DEST=".github/agent-logs/pr-refinement/pr-${PR_NUMBER}/${RUN_ID}.json"
          mkdir -p "$(dirname "$DEST")"
          cp "$LOG_PATH" "$DEST"
          git add "$DEST"
          git commit -m "agent log: PR #${PR_NUMBER} refinement run ${RUN_ID}"
          git push origin "$BRANCH"

1	name: Junior Developer agent
2	
3	on:
4	  workflow_dispatch:
5	    inputs:
6	      ticket_key:
7	        description: 'Jira ticket key (e.g. KAN-42)'
8	        required: true
9	        type: string
10	      max_turns:
11	        description: 'Override --max-turns (default 5). Use a small value to exercise the stop condition.'
12	        required: false
13	        type: string
14	        default: '5'
15	
16	jobs:
17	  implement:
18	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
19	    timeout-minutes: 90
20	    permissions:
21	      contents: write
22	      pull-requests: write
23	      id-token: write
24	      actions: write
25	    steps:
26	      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
27	        with:
28	          fetch-depth: 0
29	          # PAT with workflows:write so the agent can push branches that
30	          # add or modify .github/workflows/*. Falls back to GITHUB_TOKEN
31	          # when the PAT isn't configured (workflow-file changes will then
32	          # be rejected at push time, by design).
33	          token: ${{ secrets.DEV_PAT || secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
34	
35	      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
36	        with:
37	          node-version: '20'
38	
39	      - name: Install jira CLI
40	        run: bash scripts/install-jira-cli.sh
41	
42	      - name: Put bin/ on PATH
43	        run: echo "$PWD/bin" >> "$GITHUB_PATH"
44	
45	      - name: Write jira config
46	        env:
47	          JIRA_SERVER: ${{ vars.JIRA_SERVER }}
48	          JIRA_LOGIN: ${{ vars.JIRA_LOGIN }}
49	          JIRA_PROJECT_KEY: ${{ vars.JIRA_PROJECT_KEY }}
50	          JIRA_BOARD_ID: ${{ vars.JIRA_BOARD_ID }}
51	        run: |
52	          set -euo pipefail
53	          : "${JIRA_SERVER:?JIRA_SERVER repo variable is required}"
54	          : "${JIRA_LOGIN:?JIRA_LOGIN repo variable is required}"
55	          : "${JIRA_PROJECT_KEY:?JIRA_PROJECT_KEY repo variable is required}"
56	          mkdir -p .jira
57	          cat > .jira/config.yml <<EOF
58	          installation: Cloud
59	          server: ${JIRA_SERVER}
60	          login: ${JIRA_LOGIN}
61	          project:
62	              key: ${JIRA_PROJECT_KEY}
63	              type: classic
64	          board:
65	              id: ${JIRA_BOARD_ID:-0}
66	          EOF
67	
68	      - name: Install Claude Code CLI
69	        run: npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
70	
71	      - name: Configure git user
72	        run: |
73	          git config --global user.name "github-actions[bot]"
74	          git config --global user.email "41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
75	
76	      - name: Create feature branch
77	        env:
78	          TICKET_KEY: ${{ inputs.ticket_key }}
79	        run: |
80	          set -euo pipefail
81	          git fetch --depth=1 origin main
82	          git checkout -B "junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}" origin/main
83	
84	      - id: prompt
85	        env:
86	          TICKET_KEY: ${{ inputs.ticket_key }}
87	        run: |
88	          {
89	            echo 'PROMPT<<PROMPT_EOF'
90	            echo "TICKET_KEY: ${TICKET_KEY}"
91	            echo "DATE: $(date -u +%F)"
92	            echo
93	            cat agents/junior-dev/prompt.md
94	            echo PROMPT_EOF
95	          } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
96	
97	      - id: claude
98	        name: Run Claude (headless)
99	        env:
100	          CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
101	          CONTEXT7_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.CONTEXT7_API_KEY }}
102	          JIRA_CONFIG_FILE: ${{ github.workspace }}/.jira/config.yml
103	          JIRA_API_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.JIRA_API_TOKEN }}
104	          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.DEV_PAT || secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
105	          PROMPT: ${{ steps.prompt.outputs.PROMPT }}
106	          MAX_TURNS: ${{ inputs.max_turns }}
107	        run: |
108	          set +e
109	          claude -p "$PROMPT" \
110	            --permission-mode acceptEdits \
111	            --max-turns "$MAX_TURNS" \

file.content:
name: Junior Developer agent

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      ticket_key:
        description: 'Jira ticket key (e.g. KAN-42)'
        required: true
        type: string
      max_turns:
        description: 'Override --max-turns (default 5). Use a small value to exercise the stop condition.'
        required: false
        type: string
        default: '5'

jobs:
  implement:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 90
    permissions:
      contents: write
      pull-requests: write
      id-token: write
      actions: write
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
          # PAT with workflows:write so the agent can push branches that
          # add or modify .github/workflows/*. Falls back to GITHUB_TOKEN
          # when the PAT isn't configured (workflow-file changes will then
          # be rejected at push time, by design).
          token: ${{ secrets.DEV_PAT || secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'

      - name: Install jira CLI
        run: bash scripts/install-jira-cli.sh

      - name: Put bin/ on PATH
        run: echo "$PWD/bin" >> "$GITHUB_PATH"

      - name: Write jira config
        env:
          JIRA_SERVER: ${{ vars.JIRA_SERVER }}
          JIRA_LOGIN: ${{ vars.JIRA_LOGIN }}
          JIRA_PROJECT_KEY: ${{ vars.JIRA_PROJECT_KEY }}
          JIRA_BOARD_ID: ${{ vars.JIRA_BOARD_ID }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          : "${JIRA_SERVER:?JIRA_SERVER repo variable is required}"
          : "${JIRA_LOGIN:?JIRA_LOGIN repo variable is required}"
          : "${JIRA_PROJECT_KEY:?JIRA_PROJECT_KEY repo variable is required}"
          mkdir -p .jira
          cat > .jira/config.yml <<EOF
          installation: Cloud
          server: ${JIRA_SERVER}
          login: ${JIRA_LOGIN}
          project:
              key: ${JIRA_PROJECT_KEY}
              type: classic
          board:
              id: ${JIRA_BOARD_ID:-0}
          EOF

      - name: Install Claude Code CLI
        run: npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code

      - name: Configure git user
        run: |
          git config --global user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config --global user.email "41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"

      - name: Create feature branch
        env:
          TICKET_KEY: ${{ inputs.ticket_key }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          git fetch --depth=1 origin main
          git checkout -B "junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}" origin/main

      - id: prompt
        env:
          TICKET_KEY: ${{ inputs.ticket_key }}
        run: |
          {
            echo 'PROMPT<<PROMPT_EOF'
            echo "TICKET_KEY: ${TICKET_KEY}"
            echo "DATE: $(date -u +%F)"
            echo
            cat agents/junior-dev/prompt.md
            echo PROMPT_EOF
          } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - id: claude
        name: Run Claude (headless)
        env:
          CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
          CONTEXT7_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.CONTEXT7_API_KEY }}
          JIRA_CONFIG_FILE: ${{ github.workspace }}/.jira/config.yml
          JIRA_API_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.JIRA_API_TOKEN }}
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.DEV_PAT || secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          PROMPT: ${{ steps.prompt.outputs.PROMPT }}
          MAX_TURNS: ${{ inputs.max_turns }}
        run: |
          set +e
          claude -p "$PROMPT" \
            --permission-mode acceptEdits \
            --max-turns "$MAX_TURNS" \
            --output-format stream-json \
            --verbose \
            --allowedTools "Bash(jira issue view:*),Bash(bin/jira issue view:*),Bash(git add:*),Bash(git commit:*),Bash(git status:*),Bash(git diff:*),Bash(git log:*),Bash(git push:*),Bash(git rev-parse:*),Bash(git remote:*),Bash(git config --get:*),Bash(git config --list),Bash(git branch:*),Bash(git show:*),Bash(gh pr create:*),Bash(gh pr view:*),Bash(gh auth status),Bash(gh api repos/:*),Bash(npm test:*),Bash(npm run:*),Bash(ls:*),Bash(cat:*),Bash(printenv:*),Bash(env),Read,Glob,Grep,Edit,Write,mcp__context7__resolve-library-id,mcp__context7__query-docs,WebSearch,WebFetch" \
            > stream.jsonl
          rc=$?
          echo "claude_exit=$rc" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          # Always succeed; downstream steps handle non-zero claude exit.
          exit 0

      - name: Convert log to JSON array
        if: always()
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          if [ -s stream.jsonl ]; then
            jq -s '.' stream.jsonl > execution.json
          else
            echo '[]' > execution.json
          fi

      - name: Open fallback Draft PR if agent didn't
        if: always()
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.DEV_PAT || secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          TICKET_KEY: ${{ inputs.ticket_key }}
          CLAUDE_EXIT: ${{ steps.claude.outputs.claude_exit }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail

          # Skip if the agent already opened a PR for this branch.
          if gh pr view "junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}" --json url >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            echo "PR already exists for junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}; skipping fallback."
            exit 0
          fi

          # Need at least one commit beyond origin/main to have something to push.
          commits=$(git rev-list --count "origin/main..HEAD" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
          if [ "$commits" -eq 0 ]; then
            echo "No commits on branch; nothing to push."
            exit 0
          fi

          # Push the branch (no-op if the agent already pushed).
          git push -u origin "junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}" || true

          gh pr create --draft \
            --base main \
            --head "junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}" \
            --title "[WIP] [${TICKET_KEY}] junior-dev exited before opening PR" \
            --body "Junior-dev agent run terminated before opening a PR itself (claude exit code ${CLAUDE_EXIT:-unknown}; likely \`--max-turns\` cap or runtime error). Branch holds whatever the agent committed. See \`.github/agent-logs/junior-dev/\` for the run log."

      - name: Commit execution log to main
        if: always() && hashFiles('execution.json') != ''
        env:
          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
        run: |
          set -e
          git fetch --depth=1 origin main
          git checkout -B main origin/main
          DATE="$(date -u +%F)"
          DEST=".github/agent-logs/junior-dev/${DATE}/${RUN_ID}.json"
          mkdir -p "$(dirname "$DEST")"
          cp execution.json "$DEST"
          git add "$DEST"
          git commit -m "agent log: junior-dev run ${RUN_ID}"
          git push origin main

      - name: Trigger agent improver
        if: always() && hashFiles('execution.json') != ''
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
        run: |
          DATE="$(date -u +%F)"
          gh workflow run agent-improver.yml \
            -f agent_name=junior-dev \
            -f log_path=".github/agent-logs/junior-dev/${DATE}/${RUN_ID}.json" \
            -f log_ref=main
